plan.context:
The homepage hero card (`src/components/marketing/HeroCard.astro`) is the first thing a visitor sees on `website.beings.com`. The current implementation uses ad-hoc font sizes, hardcoded colours, and inline shadow rules that bypass the brand-token SSOT defined in `src/styles/brand-tokens.css`.
This spec replaces the hardcoded values with brand-token references and extracts the headline/subhead typography into a small, reusable type scale that other marketing sections (`FeatureGrid`, `CTASection`, `PricingTable`) will adopt in follow-up specs. The result is one place to change marketing typography, full dark-mode support without component-level conditionals, and Lighthouse-friendly CSS (no redundant declarations across sections).
Reference materials: - docs/architecture.md §7 (Brand Token System) - docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml <component name="HeroMain" priority="P0"> - src/styles/brand-tokens.css - .cursor/rules/brand.mdc (anti-patterns)
plan.problem-statement:
The hero card currently:
1. Hardcodes `#1A1D2E` for the headline colour instead of `var(--color-text-primary)`. 2. Uses `font-size: 56px` literally instead of a token like `--font-size-hero` (which does not yet exist and should be added). 3. Applies a custom `box-shadow: 0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.12)` instead of referencing `--elevation-3`. 4. Defines a one-off rounded-corner value (`border-radius: 20px`) that sits between `--radius-lg` (16px) and `--radius-xl` (28px), breaking the M3 scale. 5. Does not visually adapt to dark mode because all colour values are baked into component CSS rather than driven by token references.
Together these block consistent dark-mode rendering, force every typography tweak to touch the component, and make the section the canary for visual drift between the marketing site and the product.
plan.goal:
After this spec ships:
1. Every visible style in `HeroCard.astro` resolves through a brand token. 2. `--font-size-hero` and `--line-height-hero` exist in `brand-tokens.css` and are used by the headline. 3. Dark mode is correct without any component-level conditional logic. 4. Lighthouse accessibility audit shows no contrast regressions in light or dark mode. 5. The component compiles cleanly under `npm run build` and renders identically (to the eye) in light mode and correctly in dark mode.
plan.thesis:
Tokens are the cheapest unit of consistency. Centralising headline typography removes the temptation for each new marketing section to invent its own scale. The hero is the highest-traffic, highest-leverage place to anchor the type system because every subsequent section will reference back to "the hero typography."
Alternative considered: introduce a `Headline` component that bundles font-size + line-height + colour. Rejected for now — the Astro components are small enough that direct token references in component CSS are clearer than another wrapper layer. Revisit if more than three marketing sections need the same scale and start drifting.
plan.invariants: - `src/styles/brand-tokens.css` remains the SSOT for all visual values. - No hex colours, raw px sizes, or custom `box-shadow` declarations in component code after this spec. - The site builds with `output: 'static'` (no SSR introduced). - No new client directives added to the hero — it remains a fully static `.astro` component. - PRD priorities are not changed; this spec implements an existing P0 component, not a new one.

phase.name: add-hero-typography-tokens
phase.description: Add hero-scale typography tokens to the brand-tokens stylesheet. Tokens must be defined in both the light-mode `:root` block and the `[data-theme="dark"]` block where the values differ. Hero font size and line height are the same in both modes; this phase only adds tokens, no usage changes yet.
phase.acceptance-criteria: - `grep -E '--font-size-hero|--line-height-hero' src/styles/brand-tokens.css` returns the two new lines. - `npm run build` succeeds. - No other files are modified in this phase.

phase.name: refactor-hero-card-to-tokens
phase.description: Replace every hardcoded visual value in `HeroCard.astro` with a token reference. No layout changes, no copy changes. Visual output must be pixel-equivalent in light mode and correct in dark mode.
phase.change: - Replace `color: #1A1D2E` (headline) with `color: var(--color-text-primary)`. - Replace subhead colour with `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`. - Replace `font-size: 56px` with `font-size: var(--font-size-hero)`. - Replace inline `line-height: 1.1` with `line-height: var(--line-height-hero)`. - Replace `border-radius: 20px` with `border-radius: var(--radius-lg)` (16px — closest legal token on the M3 scale). - Replace `box-shadow: 0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.12)` with `box-shadow: var(--elevation-3)`. - Replace any spacing values with tokens from the `--space-*` scale. - Keep component props, slots, and markup structure unchanged.
phase.acceptance-criteria: - `rg '#[0-9A-Fa-f]{3,6}' src/components/marketing/HeroCard.astro` returns zero hits. - `rg 'px\)' src/components/marketing/HeroCard.astro` (raw px in shadow or radius values) returns zero hits. - `npm run build` succeeds. - `npm run preview` shows the hero rendering correctly in light mode. - Adding `data-theme="dark"` to `<html>` (via DevTools) shows legible text and the card silhouette inverted correctly.

phase.name: lighthouse-and-a11y-pass
phase.description: Run a Lighthouse pass against the homepage in both modes and confirm no regressions. Document any contrast or motion findings, then either fix them in this phase or surface them as follow-up specs.
phase.change: Append a `<phase-report id="P3">` element to this spec's `<changelog>` summarising the Lighthouse scores observed and any follow-up actions required.
phase.acceptance-criteria: - Lighthouse Performance ≥ 95. - Lighthouse Accessibility ≥ 95 in both light and dark modes. - No new motion-related accessibility violations (`prefers-reduced-motion` honoured). - Any new findings are filed as follow-up specs in `planning/`.

plan.anti-patterns: DO NOT introduce a `Headline` React component as part of this spec. Token references in component CSS are sufficient until at least three marketing sections share the same scale. DO NOT modify any other marketing components in this spec. Follow-up specs will migrate `FeatureGrid`, `CTASection`, and `PricingTable`. DO NOT add a new client directive to the hero. It remains static. DO NOT change copy, layout, or the visual hierarchy. Token swaps only. DO NOT introduce new dependencies.

references.reference: docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml — the <component name="HeroMain" priority="P0"> entry defines the canonical hero contract.
docs/architecture.md §7 (Brand Token System) — the SSOT discipline this spec enforces.
src/styles/brand-tokens.css — the file edited in P1.
.cursor/rules/brand.mdc — anti-patterns enforced by this spec (no over-rounding, no heavy shadows, no glassmorphism).

plan.changelog: v1 (2026-05-15): Initial template. Demonstrates the schema with a realistic P0 component refactor — replacing hardcoded visual values in HeroCard with brand-token references — and adds two new typography tokens upstream.
